title: ToString() method should not return null
description: "ToString()" method should not return nullAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcweWhy is this an issue?More InfoCallingToString()on an object should always return astring. Thus,overriding the ToString methodshould never returnnull, as it breaks the method’s implicit contract, and as a result the consumer’s expectations.public override string ToString () { if (this.collection.Count == 0) { return null; // Noncompliant } else { // ... } }A better alternative is to use theString.Emptybuilt-in field.public override string ToString () { if (this.collection.Count == 0) { return string.Empty; } else { // ..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: ToString() method should not return null - use string.Empty instead

never_trigger_on: ToString methods that don't return null
logic.reasoning: ToString() should always return a valid string. Returning null breaks the implicit contract and causes NullReferenceExceptions for callers. This rule detects ToString() override methods that explicitly return null.

should_trigger: public override string ToString() { return null; }
public override string ToString() { if (empty) return null; return value; }
public override string ToString() { return condition ? value : null; }
should_not_trigger: public override string ToString() { return string.Empty; }
